system_prompt_verbatim: You are an isolated benchmark agent. The only valid scope of your work is the current working directory. Do not read or write any path outside the cwd. Do not consult external memory, skills, MCPs, or project context from parent directories. Treat the task description below as the sole specification. Do not ask questions; produce the requested artifact(s) directly. When the task is complete, stop.

args.description: Read lines around Penrith critical alert code

tool_returns.detail:
The historical ledger of Riverstone was formally recorded in the spring 1980 catalogue along with seven adjacent bulletin entries covering the historical oversight period.

The Penrith critical alert code is AL-3318, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.

According to the 1991 field report compiled by the regional bureau, the rotation index for Elwick stood at 732.4 units after the winter survey, with seasonal variations noted in subsequent summary addenda.

The summary inventory of Corwick was formally recorded in the summer 2012 catalogue along with seven adjacent compendium entries covering the summary oversight period.

args.description: Read lines around Quenwick critical alert code

tool_returns.detail:
Public records show the Brenswick summary office processed routine filings in 2004 with no eastern exceptions recorded among the 701.9 entries logged in the summer bulletin for inventory archives.

The Quenwick critical alert code is AL-6741, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.

According to the 1991 field report compiled by the regional bureau, the allocation index for Kelwick stood at 140.8 units after the spring survey, with northern variations noted in subsequent catalogue addenda.

Regional observers in Marrowfen noted a perennial pattern of manifest during the spring 1990 cycle that prompted a sectional addendum filed by the inventory secretariat for archival reference.

args.description: Read lines around Riverstone critical alert code

tool_returns.detail:
The marginal frequency working group reviewed Hallow entries in their 1997 session and approved 352.4 routine ledger updates pending the winter compilation of the consolidated record.

The Riverstone critical alert code is AL-5582, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.

According to the 2012 field report compiled by the regional bureau, the dispatch index for Quenwick stood at 26.0 units after the autumn survey, with consolidated variations noted in subsequent ledger addenda.

The winter 2015 edition catalogued the Tornmoor seasonal inventory systematically alongside inventory cross-references and notes on the northern reorganisation completed during the previous reporting interval.

args.description: Read lines around Storra critical alert code

tool_returns.detail:
A summary of the 1990 Ystwyth register activity appeared in the spring compendium, listing 197.3 bulletin entries cross-indexed against the consolidated oversight register from the prior cycle.

The Storra critical alert code is AL-9911, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.

According to the 2004 field report compiled by the regional bureau, the schedule index for Saltmoor stood at 204.0 units after the autumn survey, with regional variations noted in subsequent roster addenda.

Field surveys conducted in winter 2001 placed the Brenthal perennial frequency measure at moderate levels with 439.7 as the reported central tendency for register sub-categories under continued review.

args.description: Read lines around Tornmoor critical alert code

tool_returns.detail:
The Wenmoor index board issued a perennial bulletin for the 2005 cycle outlining the summer review of index entries flagged for northern reassessment under the standing protocol.

The Tornmoor critical alert code is AL-7740, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.

Field notes from summer 2006 include observations on the Halewick index cycle and ancillary compendium markers that informed the auxiliary review prepared by the regional secretariat.

The historical inventory of Greylock was formally recorded in the winter 2016 catalogue along with seven adjacent compendium entries covering the perennial oversight period.

model_output: Now let me create the joined string with the extracted values:

args.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import hashlib

# Extract the (entity, value) pairs from the confirmed entries
pairs = [
    ("Penrith", "AL-3318"),
    ("Quenwick", "AL-6741"),
    ("Riverstone", "AL-5582"),
    ("Storra", "AL-9911"),
    ("Tornmoor", "AL-7740")
]

# Sort alphabetically by entity name
pairs.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])

# Join with | separator (no whitespace)
joined = "|".join(f"{entity}={value}" for entity, value in pairs)
print("Joined string:", joined)

# Compute SHA-1 hash
hash_result = hashlib.sha1(joined.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
print("SHA-1:", hash_result)
EOF
args.description: Extract pairs, sort, join, and compute SHA-1
